items.title: Arsenal's Nwaneri joins Marseille on loan
items.summary: Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri joins French club Marseille on loan for the remainder of the season.

items.summary: Arsenal are exploring the possibility of launching a summer move for Atletico Madrid forward Julián Álvarez, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Arteta warns Arsenal face 'lethal' Man United
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has warned Arsenal must guard against a Manchester United side "lethal" on transitions in Sunday's Premier League clash at Emirates Stadium.

items.title: Sources: L'pool, Robertson in talks with Spurs
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have entered talks with Liverpool over a potential deal for defender Andy Robertson, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Spurs in Robertson talks, Villa close on Abraham, Nwaneri joins Marseille on loan: football news – live
items.summary: ⚽ The latest football news heading into the weekend ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail David This weekend’s Premier League games are the reverse fixtures of the season openers, back in sun-soaked August. Looking back, the most jarring result was Nottingham Forest 3-1 Brentford, with the teams taking very different trajectories since then. “It was certainly a tough day for us all, but it is pretty obvious to see the development that we have made in the months since that, and I am delighted with the progress we have made,” Brentford manager Keith Andrews said yesterday. Continue reading...

items.title: Guardiola zit niet te wachten op excuses van Haaland
items.summary: Pep Guardiola vindt de excuses van Erling Haaland overbodig. Waar de doelpunten eerder dit seizoen als rijpe appelen vielen, staat de Noorse spits inmiddels alweer even droog. Daar heeft hij zich voor geëxcuseerd.

items.title: IndyCar champion Palou ordered to pay McLaren £9m for breach of contract
items.summary: Four-time IndyCar champion Alex Palou on Friday was ordered to pay McLaren more than £9m in the breach of contract suit the team filed when the Spaniard backed out of two different deals with the racing team.

items.title: Dit is NEC van plan in de laatste transferweken
items.summary: NEC zag de selectie deze winter een stuk smaller worden. Met het verhuren van Rober González aan Real Zaragoza komt het aantal vertrekkers op zeven, terwijl de Nijmegenaren nog niemand aantrokken. Nog niemand, want NEC wil zich voor de deadline op 3 februari versterken. Directeur spelerszaken Carlos Aalbers vertelt over de stand van zaken.

items.title: Arsenal-talent Nwaneri wordt ploeggenoot Timber bij Marseille
items.summary: Ethan Nwaneri maakt de overstap naar Olympique Marseille. Het achttienjarige toptalent van Arsenal wordt voor de rest van het seizoen verhuurd aan de Franse club. Nwaneri wordt zodoende ploeggenoot van Quinten Timber .

items.title: 'A big blow' - Grealish has stress fracture
items.summary: There is no timescale on on-loan Everton winger Jack Grealish's recovery from a stress fracture in his foot, says manager David Moyes.

items.title: Utrecht deelt details: drie medewerkers en speurhond gewond, veel geweld en schade
items.summary: FC Utrecht heeft vrijdagmiddag een statement over het duel met RC Genk naar buiten gebracht. Daarin trad de Europa League-deelnemer in detail over de door Genk-supporters veroorzaakte chaos. Tevens loopt er een onderzoek.

items.title: McIlroy seven shots back as Reed leads at halfway stage in Dubai
items.summary: Rory McIlroy is seven shots adrift of leader Patrick Reed at the halfway stage of the Hero Dubai Desert Classic as he aims to win the event for a third time in four years.

items.title: Veel speeltijd voor Ajax-jonkies onder Grim
items.summary: Ajax-jonkies krijgen veel speeltijd onder trainer Fred Grim. Cijferman Bart Frouws dook in de data van de Amsterdammers en vertelt erover in de podcast Scorebordjournalistiek.

items.title: 'Thrown to the sharks' - Arsenal teenager Nwaneri completes Marseille loan
items.summary: Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri has completed a loan deal to Marseille until the end of the season.

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings, a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.summary: Manchester United will look to upset Arsenal and Real Madrid face a tough test at Villarreal.

items.title: Fans’ anger after Braga flop shows Forest honeymoon is over for Dyche
items.summary: Initial optimism has faded after arrival of former Burnley manager, who needs to get more from a struggling squad There was plenty for Sean Dyche to stew on as he marched off the pitch at the Estádio Municipal de Braga with the frustrations of the Nottingham Forest supporters ringing in his ears. The boos were not universal, but fans who follow the team over land and sea are usually a decent barometer of the prevailing feeling. By the end of a sodden, joyless night in northern Portugal, it felt like false advertising, possibly one for trading standards. It wasn’t supposed to be like this? Dyche cannot be blamed for the dismal conditions but he and the players can be for a listless performance. Beyond a likely Europa League playoff, which will mean another away day to squeeze into the schedule, fans are seemingly concerned where this is all going. Forest were devoid of sharp edges and not only in attack, where the absence of a bona fide striker was painfully apparent. It was a miserable night encapsulated by second-half events: Morgan Gibbs-White’s penalty miss, a Ryan Yates own goal 54 seconds later, Dan Ndoye being booked for simulation and Elliot Anderson being sent off. Continue reading...

items.title: Spurs open talks to sign Liverpool's Robertson
items.summary: Tottenham enter into talks with Liverpool as they look to secure a deal for left-back Andy Robertson.

items.title: Chelsea’s Alyssa Thompson: ‘I think of this as like going away for college like all my friends did’
items.summary: Blues’ record signing on adapting to English football culture, the challenge of leaving family behind and being well taught by Sonia Bompastor Alyssa Thompson is no stranger to the limelight. Despite being only 21, her undeniable natural talent and eye‑catching career have propelled her into the headlines ever since her hometown club, Angel City, made her the first pick in the 2023 National Women’s Soccer League (NWSL) draft. The past few months, however, have provided the USA international with a different challenge altogether. A high-profile deadline day move to the English champions, Chelsea, in September meant leaving her family and the comforts of Los Angeles and testing herself overseas. Continue reading...

items.title: Wolves reject £33m Leeds bid for Strand Larsen
items.summary: Leeds United fail with a bid to sign Wolves striker Jorgen Strand Larsen.
